texto_base:
For generations, homework has been treated as an unquestionable part of school life, a nightly ritual as automatic as attending class itself. Yet a growing number of educators and researchers are beginning to ask a question that would have seemed almost heretical a few decades ago: does homework actually help students learn, or has it simply become a habit nobody has bothered to question?

The traditional argument in favor of homework is straightforward enough. Practice, the reasoning goes, reinforces what students learn in class, and completing assignments independently builds discipline and responsibility. On the surface, this sounds reasonable. But a closer look at the research reveals a far messier picture. Several studies comparing students in countries with heavy homework loads to those with minimal homework have found no clear, consistent advantage in academic performance for the former group. If anything, some of the highest-performing education systems in the world assign remarkably little homework, particularly in the earlier school years.

What homework does appear to do reliably, however, is consume time and generate stress. Students already spend six or seven hours a day in structured instruction; adding two or three more hours of solitary work at home leaves little room for the kind of unstructured play, family time, sleep, and rest that developmental psychologists consistently link to healthy growth. It is difficult to escape the conclusion that, for many families, homework functions less as a tool for learning and more as a source of nightly conflict between exhausted parents and equally exhausted children.

None of this is to suggest that all independent practice is worthless. Reading for pleasure, working through a genuinely engaging project, or occasionally reviewing material before a test can all have real value. The problem lies not with practice itself but with the reflexive assumption that more homework automatically means more learning, an assumption that rarely survives serious scrutiny once the actual evidence is examined.

Rather than abolishing homework outright, schools might do better to rethink its purpose entirely. Assignments should be purposeful, reasonably brief, and directly connected to what happens in the classroom, rather than existing simply because that is what schools have always done. Until educators are willing to ask harder questions about what homework is actually accomplishing, students will likely continue completing it out of habit rather than genuine educational necessity, and the nightly battle over unfinished worksheets will remain a fixture of family life for little clear benefit.

preguntas.enunciado: Based on the text, what can be inferred about the author's view of countries with minimal homework?

opciones.A: The author sees them as evidence that heavy homework loads are not necessary for academic success
opciones.B: The author believes these countries have lower educational standards overall
opciones.C: The author thinks these countries should assign even less homework than they currently do

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor cita estos países como ejemplos de alto rendimiento con poca tarea, usándolos como evidencia contra la necesidad de cargas pesadas de tarea.

B.explicacion: Contradice el tono del texto, que presenta a estos países como ejemplos de alto rendimiento, no de bajo nivel educativo.

C.explicacion: Extiende la postura del autor más allá de lo que el texto realmente afirma.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's main purpose in writing this text?

opciones.A: To provide a complete history of homework practices around the world
opciones.B: To challenge the common assumption that homework is always beneficial and suggest a more thoughtful approach
opciones.C: To argue that all schools should eliminate homework immediately

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto cuestiona la creencia tradicional sobre la tarea y propone repensar su propósito, sin pedir su eliminación total.

A.explicacion: El texto no ofrece un recuento histórico exhaustivo de la práctica.

C.explicacion: El texto sugiere repensar el propósito de la tarea, no eliminarla por completo de inmediato, como se aclara explícitamente al final.

preguntas.enunciado: Which word best describes the author's tone toward the traditional justification for homework?

opciones.A: enthusiastic
opciones.B: indifferent
opciones.C: skeptical

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor cuestiona repetidamente la evidencia detrás del argumento tradicional, mostrando una actitud de duda razonada, no de rechazo emocional ni indiferencia.

A.explicacion: Es el tono opuesto al que realmente muestra el texto.

B.explicacion: El autor claramente tiene una postura definida y argumentada, no es indiferente al tema.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by describing homework as 'a nightly ritual as automatic as attending class itself'?

opciones.A: That homework is rarely questioned, simply accepted as routine without real justification
opciones.B: That homework should be treated with the same importance as attending class
opciones.C: That most students complete their homework every single night without exception

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La comparación con un 'ritual automático' sugiere que la tarea se acepta por costumbre, no porque se haya justificado con evidencia.

B.explicacion: Invierte el sentido crítico de la frase, que cuestiona la costumbre en vez de respaldarla.

C.explicacion: Se enfoca en la frecuencia literal de completar tareas, no en la crítica implícita sobre la falta de cuestionamiento.

preguntas.enunciado: Based on the text, what would the author most likely support?

opciones.A: A complete ban on any kind of academic work outside of school
opciones.B: Homework assignments that are short, purposeful, and clearly connected to classroom learning
opciones.C: Longer school days that eliminate the need for homework entirely

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El último párrafo propone explícitamente tareas breves, con propósito claro y conectadas con lo visto en clase.

A.explicacion: El texto aclara explícitamente que no todo trabajo independiente carece de valor.

C.explicacion: Relacionado con reestructurar el tiempo escolar, pero el texto no propone alargar la jornada escolar.

texto_base:
Walk into almost any shopping mall today and you will find racks of clothing priced so low that buying five shirts costs less than a single meal at a decent restaurant. This is the world of fast fashion, an industry model built on producing enormous volumes of cheap, trend-driven clothing at a pace that would have seemed unimaginable just a few decades ago. While the low prices may feel like a straightforward win for consumers, the true cost of this business model is simply being paid elsewhere, often far from the eyes of the people buying the clothes.

Consider first the environmental toll. Textile production consumes staggering quantities of water, and the chemical dyes used in manufacturing frequently end up polluting rivers near factories, with consequences that fall disproportionately on communities that have no say in the industry decisions affecting their water supply. Add to this the sheer volume of textile waste generated when garments, designed to be worn only a handful of times before falling apart or falling out of style, are discarded in landfills at a staggering rate.

Then there is the human cost. Behind the impossibly low price tags lies a supply chain frequently built on labor practices that would be illegal in the countries where the clothes are ultimately sold. Workers, many of them women in developing countries, often labor for long hours in unsafe conditions for wages that fall far short of a living income. Fast fashion companies rarely deny this reality outright; instead, they tend to bury it beneath vague promises of "ongoing improvement" that somehow never seem to arrive on any meaningful timeline.

Defenders of fast fashion sometimes argue that cheap clothing democratizes fashion, allowing people with limited budgets to participate in current trends rather than being excluded by high prices. There is a kernel of truth here, but it conveniently ignores the fact that clothing itself has become so disposable that the supposed benefit barely outlasts a single season before the item ends up in a landfill anyway.

None of this means consumers alone bear responsibility for fixing an industry built on questionable practices; the companies profiting from this model have far more power to change it than any individual shopper. Still, every purchase sends a signal about what kind of industry gets to keep operating this way. Choosing quality over quantity, supporting brands that are transparent about their supply chains, and simply buying less are not perfect solutions, but they represent a meaningful starting point for anyone unwilling to keep quietly funding a system that treats both the planet and the people making our clothes as disposable as the garments themselves.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's main purpose in this text?

opciones.A: To provide an objective, balanced comparison of different clothing brands
opciones.B: To persuade readers that fast fashion carries hidden environmental and human costs that consumers should consider
opciones.C: To argue that shopping for clothes should be banned entirely

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto expone de forma argumentativa los costos ambientales y humanos ocultos de la moda rápida, buscando persuadir al lector.

A.explicacion: El texto claramente toma una postura crítica, no es una comparación objetiva y neutral.

C.explicacion: Exagera la postura real del autor, que pide consumo consciente, no una prohibición total.

preguntas.enunciado: What can be inferred about the author's opinion of fast fashion companies' promises of 'ongoing improvement'?

opciones.A: The author believes these companies have already solved most of their labor problems
opciones.B: The author thinks these promises are illegal in most countries
opciones.C: The author is skeptical that these promises lead to real, timely change

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La frase 'somehow never seem to arrive on any meaningful timeline' revela una clara desconfianza hacia esas promesas.

A.explicacion: Contradice directamente el tono crítico del párrafo.

B.explicacion: Confunde las prácticas laborales cuestionables, que sí se describen como posiblemente ilegales en otros países, con las promesas de mejora en sí.

preguntas.enunciado: How would you best describe the author's tone when discussing companies' promises of improvement?

opciones.A: sarcastic/doubtful
opciones.B: hopeful
opciones.C: neutral

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El uso de frases como 'somehow never seem to arrive' revela un tono de ironía y duda hacia esas promesas.

B.explicacion: Es el tono opuesto al que realmente muestra el texto.

C.explicacion: El lenguaje empleado muestra una actitud claramente crítica, no neutral.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by saying the 'benefit barely outlasts a single season'?

opciones.A: That fashion trends only last for one season each year
opciones.B: That the affordability of fast fashion is undermined by how quickly the clothing becomes unusable or outdated
opciones.C: That customers should buy new clothes every season to stay stylish

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La frase sugiere que la supuesta ventaja económica de la ropa barata se pierde rápidamente porque las prendas no duran.

A.explicacion: Describe un patrón cultural general, no la crítica específica sobre la calidad de la ropa.

C.explicacion: Interpreta la crítica como si fuera una recomendación de consumo, invirtiendo el sentido real de la frase.

preguntas.enunciado: Why does the author mention that consumers alone do not bear full responsibility for fixing the fast fashion industry?

opciones.A: To suggest that individual consumer choices have no real impact at all
opciones.B: To blame consumers directly for the industry's environmental damage
opciones.C: To clarify that companies hold more power to change the system, while still encouraging individual action

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor aclara que las empresas tienen más poder para cambiar el sistema, pero igual anima a los consumidores a actuar.

A.explicacion: Contradice el párrafo final, que sí anima a acciones individuales concretas.

B.explicacion: Invierte parcialmente el argumento, ya que el autor explícitamente aclara que la responsabilidad principal no recae solo en los consumidores.

texto_base:
Since remote work became widespread, a confident narrative has taken hold in many corners of the business world: working from home makes employees more productive, full stop. Surveys are cited, self-reported hours are tallied, and the conclusion seems settled. Yet a closer, more honest look at the evidence suggests the reality is considerably more complicated than this tidy narrative allows.

Part of the problem lies in how productivity gets measured in the first place. Many of the studies celebrating remote work rely heavily on employees' own self-reports, and it is hardly surprising that people freed from a daily commute and office small talk report feeling more productive, particularly during the early stages of a major shift in how they work. Self-reported feelings of productivity, however, are not the same thing as objectively measured output, and the two do not always move in the same direction.

There is also the question of what kind of work benefits from remote arrangements. Tasks requiring deep, uninterrupted focus, such as writing a report or reviewing complex data, genuinely can benefit from the reduced interruptions of a home office. But collaborative work, the kind that depends on quick clarifying questions, spontaneous brainstorming, or simply noticing a confused expression on a colleague's face, often suffers when every interaction requires scheduling a video call. Some of the most valuable exchanges in any workplace happen in the margins of formal meetings, and these margins tend to shrink considerably when teams are scattered across different homes, time zones, and schedules.

None of this means offices are automatically superior. Open-plan offices, in particular, have long been criticized for constant interruptions and a near-total lack of privacy, conditions that can be just as damaging to focused work as any home distraction. The honest conclusion is not that either arrangement wins outright, but that productivity depends heavily on the type of work being performed, the specific team involved, and how thoughtfully an organization designs its way of collaborating, regardless of location.

Companies eager to declare remote work an unqualified success, or a clear failure, are both likely ignoring evidence that contradicts their preferred conclusion. A more useful question than "which is more productive" might simply be: which kinds of tasks and teams benefit from which arrangement, and how can organizations design flexible systems that play to the actual strengths of each, rather than defending a single answer as though it applied universally to every job and every person.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's overall tone throughout the text?

opciones.A: strongly in favor of returning to full-time office work
opciones.B: dismissive of any research on remote work
opciones.C: measured and analytical, questioning oversimplified claims on both sides

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor cuestiona tanto las afirmaciones a favor del trabajo remoto como las simplificaciones a favor de la oficina, manteniendo un análisis equilibrado.

A.explicacion: El texto explícitamente rechaza declarar una superioridad absoluta de la oficina.

B.explicacion: El autor critica la metodología de ciertos estudios, pero no descarta toda la investigación sobre el tema.

preguntas.enunciado: What can be inferred about the author's view of self-reported productivity surveys?

opciones.A: The author considers them an unreliable measure of actual productivity
opciones.B: The author believes self-reported surveys are always more accurate than objective data
opciones.C: The author thinks employees deliberately lie in these surveys

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor señala que los sentimientos de productividad autoreportados no equivalen a mediciones objetivas de rendimiento.

B.explicacion: Contradice directamente lo que el texto argumenta sobre las encuestas autorreportadas.

C.explicacion: Exagera la crítica del autor, que habla de un sesgo natural en la percepción, no de mentiras intencionales.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's main purpose in this text?

opciones.A: To provide statistical data proving remote work reduces company costs
opciones.B: To challenge the simplistic idea that remote work is universally more productive than office work
opciones.C: To convince companies to abandon remote work entirely

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto cuestiona la narrativa simplificada de que el trabajo remoto siempre es más productivo, proponiendo un análisis más matizado.

A.explicacion: El texto no presenta ningún dato sobre costos empresariales.

C.explicacion: El autor no aboga por abandonar el trabajo remoto, sino por evaluar caso por caso según el tipo de tarea y equipo.

preguntas.enunciado: How does the author seem to feel about companies that declare remote work an 'unqualified success' or a 'clear failure'?

opciones.A: fully supportive of their conclusions
opciones.B: uninterested in the topic altogether
opciones.C: critical of their oversimplified reasoning

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor señala que ambas posturas extremas probablemente ignoran evidencia que contradice su conclusión preferida.

A.explicacion: Es la postura opuesta a la que realmente muestra el autor.

B.explicacion: El autor claramente está involucrado y argumenta activamente sobre el tema, no es indiferente.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by mentioning exchanges that happen 'in the margins of formal meetings'?

opciones.A: That valuable, informal communication can be lost when work becomes entirely remote and scheduled
opciones.B: That formal meetings themselves are unnecessary and should be eliminated
opciones.C: That employees waste too much time chatting during work hours

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor sugiere que las interacciones informales y espontáneas tienen valor y se pierden cuando todo debe programarse formalmente.

B.explicacion: No se sugiere en ningún momento eliminar las reuniones formales.

C.explicacion: Tergiversa el valor positivo que el autor atribuye a esas interacciones informales, presentándolas como una pérdida de tiempo.

texto_base:
When a city announces a ban on single-use plastic bags, the reaction from many shoppers is predictable: mild annoyance at having to remember reusable bags, followed by a few grumbling comments about government overreach into everyday life. This initial reaction, understandable as it may be, obscures a far more important question that rarely gets the attention it deserves: what exactly are we trading away by continuing to hand out billions of plastic bags every year for the sake of a few minutes of convenience?

The scale of the problem is difficult to overstate. A single plastic bag, used on average for only a matter of minutes before being discarded, can persist in the environment for centuries, breaking down not into harmless material but into ever-smaller fragments that infiltrate soil, waterways, and eventually the food chain itself. Marine animals frequently mistake plastic debris for food, with often fatal consequences, while the sheer volume of bags clogging drainage systems has contributed directly to flooding in several major cities during heavy rains.

Critics of plastic bag bans often argue that such policies unfairly burden low-income shoppers, who may rely on free plastic bags and cannot easily absorb the cost of purchasing reusable alternatives. This concern deserves to be taken seriously rather than dismissed, but it is ultimately an argument for designing thoughtful policy, such as providing free reusable bags to lower-income households, rather than an argument against banning plastic bags altogether. Treating a legitimate implementation concern as though it were a fatal flaw in the entire concept confuses a solvable problem with an unsolvable one.

Other critics point out that plastic bag bans alone will not solve the broader plastic pollution crisis, since bags represent only a fraction of total plastic waste. This is entirely true, yet it is a strange argument against taking action rather than an argument for it. Very few meaningful environmental improvements come from a single, comprehensive policy; they come from an accumulation of targeted measures, each addressing one identifiable source of harm. Dismissing plastic bag bans because they alone cannot solve every environmental problem sets an impossible standard that would rule out nearly every incremental policy change ever proposed.

The inconvenience of remembering a reusable bag is real, if modest, and worth acknowledging honestly rather than waving away. But when weighed against centuries of environmental persistence, genuine harm to wildlife, and a real contribution to urban flooding, that inconvenience looks less like a serious cost and considerably more like a habit we have simply grown too comfortable defending.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by calling the initial reaction to plastic bag bans 'predictable'?

opciones.A: That this reaction is common and expected, but does not reflect serious consideration of the actual issue
opciones.B: That most people are naturally opposed to any environmental policy
opciones.C: That governments always predict how citizens will react to new laws

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: Al llamar 'predecible' a la reacción inicial, el autor sugiere que es una respuesta superficial que no examina el problema real de fondo.

B.explicacion: Generaliza más allá de lo que el texto afirma específicamente sobre las bolsas plásticas.

C.explicacion: Relacionado con la idea de anticipar reacciones, pero no es lo que implica 'predictable' en este contexto específico.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's main purpose in addressing the concern about low-income shoppers?

opciones.A: To dismiss the concerns of low-income shoppers as unimportant
opciones.B: To acknowledge a valid concern while arguing it calls for better policy design, not abandoning the ban
opciones.C: To argue that low-income shoppers should pay more for groceries

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor reconoce explícitamente que la preocupación 'merece tomarse en serio', pero la usa como argumento para diseñar mejor la política, no para eliminarla.

A.explicacion: El texto explícitamente dice que el argumento merece tomarse en serio, no que sea descartable.

C.explicacion: Relacionado tangencialmente con el costo, pero distorsiona la propuesta real del autor, que es dar bolsas reutilizables gratuitas.

preguntas.enunciado: Based on the text, how would the author most likely respond to the argument that bag bans alone cannot solve plastic pollution?

opciones.A: By agreeing that this proves bag bans are pointless and should be abandoned
opciones.B: By denying that plastic bags are a significant source of pollution
opciones.C: By agreeing with the fact but rejecting it as a reason to avoid taking targeted action

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor reconoce que es 'entirely true' que las bolsas son solo una fracción del problema, pero argumenta que eso no es razón para no actuar.

A.explicacion: Contradice directamente la conclusión del autor a favor de las prohibiciones.

B.explicacion: El autor sí reconoce que las bolsas son solo una fracción del problema, pero eso no es lo mismo que negar que sean significativas.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's tone when discussing the 'inconvenience' of remembering a reusable bag?

opciones.A: acknowledging but ultimately dismissive of its significance compared to the environmental cost
opciones.B: deeply sympathetic and apologetic toward inconvenienced shoppers
opciones.C: completely indifferent to how shoppers feel about the policy

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor reconoce la molestia como 'real' pero la contrasta con daños ambientales mucho mayores, restándole importancia relativa.

B.explicacion: El autor sí reconoce brevemente la molestia, pero el tono general no es de disculpa, sino de contraste crítico.

C.explicacion: El autor sí reconoce la molestia explícitamente, no la ignora por completo.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by describing the inconvenience as 'a habit we have simply grown too comfortable defending'?

opciones.A: That plastic bags are a comfortable material to use compared to reusable alternatives
opciones.B: That people resist the change mainly out of habit, not because the inconvenience is truly significant
opciones.C: That most people are unaware plastic bags cause any environmental harm

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La frase sugiere que la resistencia al cambio se debe más a la costumbre que a un costo real, tras contrastarla con los daños ambientales descritos.

A.explicacion: Interpreta 'comfortable' de forma literal en vez de en el sentido figurado sobre la resistencia al cambio.

C.explicacion: Relacionado con el tema general del texto, pero no es lo que implica específicamente esa frase final.
